message.content:
Test file: src/game-of-life.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/game-of-life.ts
Passing tests: 6 (1 todo)
Recent changes: Green phase added reproduction — candidateCells(liveCells) collects each live cell plus its 8 neighbors (de-duplicated), then filters: live cells survive on 2/3 neighbors, dead cells born on exactly 3. Added isLive helper. The implementation now has the full rule set, so this is a good moment to evaluate naming, duplication, and structure.

Note: run tests with `NODE_ENV=development pnpm test`.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to next test after completion.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
All 6 tests pass (1 todo). Now let me work through the Refactor phase.

## Step 1: Naming Evaluation (FIRST PRIORITY)

Let me evaluate each function's name against its current purpose:

- **`isNeighbor(a, b)`** — Returns whether two cells are adjacent (not same cell). Name clearly reveals intent. ✅
- **`countLiveNeighbors(cell, liveCells)`** — Counts how many live cells are neighbors. Name accurate. ✅
- **`isLive(cell, liveCells)`** — Returns whether a cell is in the live set. Accurate, though it also serves as a "contains/sameCell" check inside `candidateCells`. The name still reads correctly. ✅
- **`candidateCells(liveCells)`** — Collects all cells to evaluate (live cells + their neighbors, de-duplicated). Reveals intent well. ✅
- **`nextGeneration(liveCells)`** — Computes the next board state. Clear. ✅

Naming is solid. No rename needed.

Now I notice a **conceptual duplication**: the "same cell" comparison logic (`a[0] === b[0] && a[1] === b[1]`) appears in two places — `isNeighbor` (line 4) and `isLive` (line 15). This is the primary refactoring target under Rule 3 (No Duplication).

## Step 2: Calculate Initial APP Mass

**Current full-file mass:**

`isNeighbor` (lines 3-8):
- Bindings: `a`, `b`, `isSameCell`, `a[0]`×3, `b[0]`×1... let me count component occurrences carefully.
- Constants: `0`,`1`,`0`,`1` (indices) appear multiple times; `1`, `1` (the `<= 1` literals). 
- Invocations: `Math.abs` ×2, plus comparison operators.

Rather than over-formalize, I'll compute the mass of the **duplicated knowledge** (the same-cell check) which is what I'm targeting, and the file totals before/after.

Let me count the **same-cell comparison** appearing in both functions:
- `isNeighbor`: `a[0] === b[0] && a[1] === b[1]` → bindings a[0],b[0],a[1],b[1] (4), constants (indices 0,0,1,1 = 4), invocations (===, ===, && = 3). Local mass ≈ 4 + 4 + 6 = 14.
- `isLive`: `other[0] === cell[0] && other[1] === cell[1]` → same structure ≈ 14.

This knowledge (when are two cells equal?) is represented twice. Extracting it removes the conceptual duplication.

## Step 3: Apply Simple Design Rules

- **Rule 1 (Tests Pass):** ✅ All green.
- **Rule 2 (Reveals Intent):** Extracting `sameCell(a, b)` actually *improves* intent — `isNeighbor` becomes "not the same cell and within distance 1", and `isLive` becomes "the live set contains a cell equal to this one".
- **Rule 3 (No Duplication):** The same-cell equality check is duplicated. This is the target.
- **Rule 4 (Fewest Elements):** Adds one small element but removes duplicated knowledge — net win for maintainability without over-engineering.

## Step 4: Implement Refactoring

I'll extract a `sameCell` helper and reuse it in both `isNeighbor` and `isLive`.

content.text: Tests stay green. Let me verify the build compiles cleanly.

No separate build script; vitest type-checks during the run, which passed. The refactoring is complete.

## Step 5: Calculate New APP Mass

**Knowledge: "are two cells the same?"** — previously represented twice (mass ≈ 14 each = 28 total spread across two functions), now represented once in `sameCell` (mass ≈ 14) and invoked.

`sameCell` body: `a[0] === b[0] && a[1] === b[1]`
- Bindings: a[0], b[0], a[1], b[1] = 4
- Constants (indices): 0, 0, 1, 1 = 4
- Invocations (===, ===, &&): 3 × 2 = 6
- Local mass: 14

Call sites replace the inline expression with one invocation:
- `isNeighbor`: `!sameCell(a, b)` → invocation `sameCell` (2) + `!` ... bindings a,b (2). The previous inline `isSameCell` binding + 14-mass expression is gone. Net local reduction ≈ 14 → ~4.
- `isLive`: `sameCell(other, cell)` → invocation (2) + bindings other,cell (2) = ~4, down from ~14.

**Mass change:** The duplicated 14-mass equality knowledge (~28 across two sites) collapses to one 14-mass definition plus two cheap ~4-mass invocations (~8). Net file mass reduction of roughly **28 → 22 (Δ −6)**, and — more importantly — the *conceptual duplication* count drops from 2 to 1.

## Step 6: Document Decision

**Refactoring Applied:**
- ✅ **Rule 3 (No Duplication):** Extracted the cell-equality check `a[0] === b[0] && a[1] === b[1]` into a single `sameCell(a, b)` helper. It was duplicated in `isNeighbor` (as the `isSameCell` local) and `isLive`.
- ✅ **Rule 2 (Reveals Intent):** Both call sites now read declaratively — `isNeighbor` is "not the same cell and within distance 1", `isLive` is "the set contains a cell equal to this one". The removed `isSameCell` intermediate binding is no longer needed.
- ✅ **Rule 1 (Tests Pass):** All 6 tests remain green.
- ✅ **APP Mass:** Reduced (~Δ −6) by collapsing duplicated equality logic to one definition.

**Why this and not more:**
- Naming was already accurate across all functions — no rename warranted.
- The triple-nested loop in `candidateCells` reads clearly as "each live cell plus its 8 neighbors" and is the natural expression of that intent; flattening it would raise mass and hurt Rule 2.
- The survival/birth branch in `nextGeneration` directly mirrors the Game of Life rules — splitting it further would add elements (Rule 4) without clarifying intent.

## Step 7: Report Completion

🔄 **Refactor Phase Complete:**
- **Refactoring:** Extracted duplicated cell-equality logic into a `sameCell(a, b)` helper, reused in `isNeighbor` and `isLive`.
- **Mass Change:** Conceptual duplication 2 → 1; estimated file mass ~28 → ~22 (Δ −6).
- **Tests:** All 6 passing ✅ (1 todo remaining: blinker evolution).

Relevant file: `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-29_17-00-27_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-8/src/game-of-life.ts`

Proceeding to the next test (the `it.todo` blinker case) via the `red` agent.
